text: Definition of Confidential Information Confidential Information includes business plans, source code, customer lists, financial information, trade secrets, and any information marked confidential
Obligations of Receiving Party The Receiving Party shall protect Confidential Information and shall not disclose it to third parties without prior written consent
Exclusions Information that is publicly available, independently developed, or lawfully obtained from a third party shall not be considered Confidential Information
Return of Information Upon written request, the Receiving Party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information
Term The confidentiality obligations shall remain in effect for five years after termination of this Agreement
Risky Clause - Broad Disclosure Right The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information to any affiliate without obtaining prior consent from the Disclosing Party
Risky Clause - Unlimited Liability Waiver The Disclosing Party waives all claims arising from unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information
Risky Clause - One-Sided Termination The Receiving Party may terminate this Agreement at any time without notice, while the Disclosing Party may not terminate the Agreement
Governing Law This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the applicable jurisdiction
Severability If any provision is held invalid, the remaining provisions shall remain in effect
